narrative:
Bart Verbruggen is a goalkeeper for the Kingdom of the Netherlands, holding an overall rating of 7.49/10. This rating places him within the top 5% of goalkeepers at this tournament. His rating range is 7.08–7.9, reflecting his match sample size.

Evidence from the World Cup shows Verbruggen records 3.692 saves per 90 minutes, highlighting his primary strength as a shot-stopper. His expected assists per 90 minutes is 0.001 at this World Cup and 0.002 at club level, indicating he is not involved in significant build-up play. Furthermore, he averages 0.231 clearances per 90 minutes, suggesting a more conservative approach in distribution.

Verbruggen serves primarily as a reliable last line of defense for his national team, with 89% of his rating derived from consistent club form. Scouts and fans should continue to watch for his shot-stopping ability and commanding presence within his area in the remaining matches. His low expected assists confirm he is not expected to contribute significantly to offensive transitions.
